rationale: Flywheel adapter passes both gates and wins the base-size ablation. Deploy flywheel-full-r8 as the new production adapter.

gate_1_targeted_improvement.verdict: PASS -- over-extraction eliminated; counter-example recall held at 1.00 (no over-suppression)

gate_2_no_regression.verdict: PASS within tolerance -- synthetic unchanged; human OOD -0.009 (mainly one parse failure), a small real tradeoff surfaced by the gate

base_size_ablation.question: does a smaller base (500) give a better flywheel tradeoff?
base_size_ablation.answer: no -- full base wins on every axis
base_size_ablation.finding: correction-integration quality depends on base quality: a stronger base integrates targeted fixes more precisely (blended 1.00 vs 0.92) and generalises better, with lower hallucination. The intuition that a smaller base gives corrections more relative weight is refuted.
